author: @htomerif
text:
hmm, not to be a buzzkill but I'm fairly certain that the reason the graphite sheet is moving is because it is transitioning from diamagnetic to paramagnetic due to the heat the same way a ferromagnetic substance does at sufficient temperature.

I dont think it would spin in some arrangement of concentric magnetic fields so much as just dip towards the side that is heated.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: htomerif don't worry about it mate - it probably is doing exactly that. Still it is interesting it does anything

replies.author: @htomerif
replies.text:
Robert Murray-Smith Do you know if the diamagnetism of that material is anisotropic with respect to the cleavage planes?  I suspect it might be, such that it is not diamagnetic when the sheets are parallel to the magnetic field, only when they are perpendicular.  Theres a lot of anisotropy of electrical resistance in that kind of thing.

It might be somewhat difficult to test.  If you crushed it into a fine powder I would expect the grains to order themselves with the magnetic field and not levitate when placed in something like a test tube to keep them contained.  since the diamagnetism is so weak though it might not overcome van der waals forces or just plain electrostatic cling even if Im wrong.

Anyway, I could possibly imagine some interesting effects if the diamagnetism was dependent on the sheet orientation.

author: @qonoximiento1
text: Maybe is not just sun light but combination of sunlight and heat, pe rhaps apply heat alone.

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: The heat from the light destabilised the crystal structure and it loses it's diamagnetic properties. The other side keeps that property so, it is not so much that it is attracted to the light but rather repulsed from the other side

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: no probs mate. I am going to try making an exfoliated form and try making a foil of that. If you were to mix the titanate in with the exfoliated graphite first then press into a foil that may be a way of getting the two to combine. I am not sure what is in there. So far I have asked for an assay and a friend of mine is waiting to get a bit so he can run it through a spectrometer and SEM. so hopefully we will have some answers soon - but these thing take a bit of time - Rob.

text:
Hi Robert
I was going to ask you to watch the latest video of Wesley (stevep1), hoping you have the right background on chemistry. watch?v=Mk3ku_dBzBM but I don't want to take you away from experimenting with what you have got right now.  As I watched the magnet move by the sunlight I was thinking: hey, maybe this can make for us the ideal pointing device (for solar panels). Like WOW, no electronics. But I am sure it will apply in many ways. Thanks for sharing, it's really appreciated.

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: This particular graphite is highly diamagnetic. The diamagnetism is probably a product of the dopants and lattice structure. The photon input will alter this an therefor alter the diamagnetic property at that point causing an imbalance in the system that attempts to compensate for and therefore we get movement. The relaxation time is very rapid so removing the input returns the equilibrium.
